[image]
COUNCIL MEETING
A G E N D A
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 5:00 P.m. - 8:00 P.m.
Council Chamber, City Hall
4949 Canada Way, Burnaby, BC
Code of Conduct - Glossary of Terms
 
1.
CALL TO ORDER
 
2.
TERRITORIAL ACKNOWLEDGEMENT
 
3.
ANNOUNCEMENTS
 

4.
ADOPTION OF AGENDA
 
4.1
Open Council Meeting Agenda for March 24, 2026
 
5.
ADOPTION OF MINUTES
 
5.1
Open Council Meeting Minutes March 10, 2026
[image] 
1.2026.03.10 Council Minutes DRAFT.pdf
5.2
Parcel Tax Roll Review Panel Minutes March 12,
2026
[image] 
1.2026.03.12 PTRRP Minutes DRAFT.pdf
6.
DELEGATIONS AND INVITED
PRESENTATIONS
 
None.

7.
ADMINISTRATIVE REPORTS
 
7.1
BHA - 2026-2030 STRATEGIC PLAN
[image] 
1.BHA - 2026-2030 Strategic Plan 2026-03-24.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - BHA 2026-2030 Strategic Plan
2026-03-24_.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing
Authority Corporation’s (BHA) 2026-2030 Strategic Plan.
7.2
UBCM 2026 MEMBERSHIP DUES
[image] 
1.UBCM 2026 Membership Dues 2026-03-24.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - UBCM 2026 Membership Invoice
2026-03-24.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Letter from the UBCM President
2026-03-24.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council approval for payment of the Union of
British Columbia Municipalities (UBCM) 2026 membership
dues.
7.3
CA - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY WATER METERING
TECHNOLOGY
[image] 
1.CA - Supply and Delivery Water Metering Technology
2026-03-24.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Universal Water Metering Stragtegy

(Updated) (Council 2024-12-02) 2026-03-24 .pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for the Supply and Delivery of Water Metering Technology.
7.4
CA - DEVELOPMENT OF MODULAR HOUSING
FOUR-PLEX UNIT AT 8315 14TH AVENUE
[image] 
1.CA - Development Modular Housing Four-Plex 8315 14th
Ave 2026-03-24.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA)
for the development of a modular four-plex housing unit as a
pilot project.
7.5
CA - HIRED EQUIPMENT SERVICES
[image] 
1.CA - HE Services - All Contractors - 2026-03-24_.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval to award nineteen
separate contracts (CA) for Hired Equipment Services.
7.6
CR - ASPHALT PAVING SERVICES - ENGINEERING
DEPARTMENT
[image] 
1.CR - Asphalt Paving Services - Engineering Department
2026-03-24.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Contract History (Asphalt Paving Services -
Engineering Department) 2026-03-24.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Council Report 2025-05-13 (Asphalt Paving
Services - Engineering Department) 2026-03-24.pdf
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal

(CR) for Asphalt Paving Services – Engineering Department.
8.
COMMITTEE REPORTS
 
8.1
PDC - AMENDED DEVELOPMENT PROPOSAL
ENQUIRY FOR 3856 AND 3870 CARRIGAN COURT
- PROPOSED AMENDED HIGH-DENSITY MIXED-
USE COMMERCIAL/RESIDENTIAL STRATA AND
RENTAL DEVELOPMENT
[image] 
1.PDC - Development Enquiry 3856 and 3870 Carrigan Court
2026-03-24.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Sketch 1, 2, Site Plan and Site Perspective
(3856 and 3870 Carrigan Crt) 2026-03-24.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to work with the
applicant towards the preparation of a suitable plan of
development, in accordance with Section 3.0 of this report, on
the understanding that a more detailed report will be submitted
for Council consideration at a later date.
8.2
PDC - REZ #24-23 - 3900 GRAND PROMENADE,
9850 AUSTIN RD, AND 9858 AND 9898 GATINEAU
PLACE - AMENDED MIXED-USE COMMERCIAL/
RESIDENTIAL DEVELOPMENT
[image] 
1.PDC - REZ 24-23 3900 Grand Promenade 2026-03-24.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Part 1 of 4 - REZ 24-23 - Sketch 1, 2, Site
Plan and Phase 1 Site Plan 2026-03-24.pdf
3.Attachment 1 - Part 2 of 4 - REZ 24-23 - Subdivision Plan

and Shadow Study Plan 2026-03-24.pdf
4.Attachment 1 - Part 3 of 4 - REZ 24-23 - Elevations
2026-03-24.pdf
5.Attachment 1 - Part 4 of 4 - REZ 24-23 - Elevations (2)
2026-03-24.pdf
6.Attachment 2 - REZ 24-23 - Development Statistics
2026-03-24.pdf
7.Attachment 3 - REZ 24-23 - Rezoning Prerequisites
2026-03-24.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #24-23
to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.
8.3
PDC - REZ #25-09 - 6535 KINGSWAY - PROPOSED
TWELVE-STOREY MIXED-USE DEVELOPMENT
[image] 
1.PDC - REZ 25-09 6535 Kingsway 2026-03-24.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Part 1 of 3 - PDC - REZ 25-09 - Sketch 1, 2,
Subdivision Plan and Site Plan (L1) 2026-03-24.pdf
3.Attachment 1 - Part 2 of 3 - REZ 25-09 - Landscape Plans
2026-03-24.pdf
4.Attachment 1 - Part 3 of 3 - REZ 25-09 - Elevations
2026-03-24.pdf
5.Attachment 2 - REZ 25-09 - Development Statistics
2026-03-24.pdf
6.Attachment 3 - REZ 25-09 - Rezoning Prerequisites
2026-03-24.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-09
to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting.
8.4
PDC - REZ #25-21 - 2757 NORLAND AVENUE -
FIVE-STOREY SELF-STORAGE FACILITY
[image] 
1.PDC - REZ 25-21 2757 Norland Avenue 2026-03-24.pdf

2.Attachment 1 - Part 1 of 2 - REZ 25-21 - Sketch 1, 2, Draft
Subdivision Plan and Storage 2026-03-24.pdf
3.Attachment 1 - Part 2 of 2 - REZ 25-21 - Site Plan and

4.Attachment 2 - REZ 25-21 - Development Statistics
2026-03-24.pdf
5.Attachment 3 - REZ 25-21 - Rezoning Prerequisites
2026-03-24.pdf
Purpose: To seek Council authorization to forward REZ #25-21

8.5
SPC - BURNABY POVERTY REDUCTION STRATEGY
[image] 
1.SPC - Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy 2026-03-24.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy
2026-03-24.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - What We Heard Report (Burnaby Poverty
Reduction Strategy) 2026-03-24.pdf

Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the Burnaby Poverty

9.
CONSENT AGENDA
 
9.1
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 1
FROM JANUARY 1 - JANUARY 31, 2026, AND
BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 2
FROM FEBRUARY 1 - FEBRUARY 28, 2026

[image] 
1.SU - Building Permit Activity Report No 01 and No 02
2026-03-24.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - Building Permit Activity Report No 01
(January) 2026-03-24.pdf
3.Attachment 2 - Building Permit Activity Report No 02
(February) 2026-03-24.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction
activity as reflected by the building permits that have been
issued for the subject period.
9.2
FMC - CITY INVESTMENTS - 2025 YEAR END
REPORT
[image] 
1.FMC - City Investments – 2025 Year End Report
2026-03-24.pdf
2.Attachment 1 - COB Investment Limites for 2026
2026-03-24.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with an update on the 2025
Investment Program and present a forecast for 2026.
9.3
FMC - SU: SIGNIFICANT CIVIC PROJECTS - MARCH
2026
[image] 
1.FMC - SU Significant Civic Projects – March 2026
2026-03-24.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with a status update (SU) on
significant civic projects.
9.4
PDC - OCP: BURNABY 2050 IMPLEMENTATION
ACTION PLAN PROJECT INITIATION

[image] 
1.PDC - OCP - Burnaby 2050 Implementation Action Plan
2026-03-24.pdf
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the Official
Community Plan (OCP) Implementation Action Plan.
10.
INFORMATION REPORTS
 
None.
11.
BYLAWS
 
11.1
FIRST AND SECOND READING
 
11.1.1
#14802 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 4, 2026 - REZ #25-13 (5058 Imperial Street)
[image] 
1.14802 Amend No. 4 2026 REZ 25-13 (5058 Imperial St).ACD
2026.pdf
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 20-storey mixed-use
multiple-family market rental residential development fronting
Imperial Street with a double-height commercial storey at
street level. The proposed development concept supersedes a
previously approved plan for a six-storey market and non-

market rental apartment building on the site approved through
REZ #20-36
(Item 8.4, PDC Report, Council February 24, 2026)
11.1.2
#14803 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 5, 2026 - REZ #25-18 (6800 Lougheed Hwy)
[image] 
1.14803 Amendment No. 5 2026 REZ 25-18 (6800 Lougheed
Hwy).ACD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to advance a Master Plan (MP) amendment to the
Burnaby Lake Village Conceptual Master Plan to establish a
revised site plan with updated height and massing in alignment
with the recently adopted OCP 2050 and associated height-
based zoning districts (R6 and CM1), as well as expanded open
space provisions for the site. No development or subdivision is
proposed through the subject MP amendment
(Item 8.5., PDC Report, Council February 24, 2026)
11.1.3
#14804 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw
No. 6, 2026 - REZ #25-20 (2209 & 2225 Springer Avenue)
[image] 
1.14804 Amendment No. 6 2026 REZ 25-20 (2209 and 2255
Springer Ave).CD 2026.pdf
Purpose: to permit the construction of a single high-rise
apartment tower of up to 49 storeys inclusive of a 9 storey
terraced podium with street fronting retail

(Item 8.6., PDC Report, Council February 24, 2026)
11.2
 FINAL ADOPTION
 
11.2.1
#14805 - Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle
Charging Meter Bylaw 2019, Amendment Bylaw No. 1,
2026
[image] 
1.14805 Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging
Meter Bylaw 2019 Amendment Bylaw No. 1 2026 (FA).pdf
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle
Charging Meter Bylaw 2019 (public EV network)
(Item 8.3., FMC Report, Council February 24, 2026)
11.2.2
#14806 - Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw,
Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026
[image] 
1.14806 Burnaby Consolidated Fees Charges Bylaw
Amendment Bylaw No. 2 2026 (FA).pdf
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges
Bylaw (public EV network)
(Item 8.3., FMC Report, Council February 24, 2026)
11.2.3
#14807 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009,

Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026
[image] 
1.14807 Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amend Bylaw
No. 2, 2026 Burnaby Parking Meter_Electric Charging Meter
(FA).pdf
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement
Bylaw 2009 (public EV network)
(Item 8.3., FMC Report, Council February 24, 2026)
12.
CORRESPONDENCE
 
The following item of correspondence was received for
Council's decision. All other items of correspondence were
published at the Council Correspondence and Public Notice
Submissions package dated March 24, 2026.
12.1
PETITION: Opposition to REZ #25-13 - 5058
Imperial Street
[image] 
1.Petition - Opposition to REZ 25-13 - 5058 Imperial
2026-03-24.pdf
2.Supplementary letter and petition received March 24,
2026.pdf
3.Attachment 1 - Correspondence re REZ 25-13 - 5058 Imperial
Street 2026-03-24.pdf
4.Attachment 2 - Petition re REZ 25-13 - 5058 Imperial Street
2026-03-24.pdf
Purpose: To provide a petition from the residents of 5051

Imperial Street and surrounding neighbours in opposition to
REZ #25-13 – 5058 Imperial Street.
13.
OTHER BUSINESS 
 
14.
RELEASE OF CLOSED MEETING
DECISIONS
 
14.1
Resolution No. C2026 - 029
 
THAT Joanne Morgan be approved as Burnaby’s 2025 Outstanding
Citizen of the Year, and that the Kushiro Cup presentation take
place at the 2026 City Appreciation Dinner on May 1, 2026;
THAT the recommendation of the report titled “2025 Citizen of the
Year – Kushiro Cup Award”, dated March 10, 2026, be released at
the March 24, 2026, Open Council meeting; and
THAT staff be authorized to contact the recipients and nominators
prior to Council’s Open meeting.
CARRIED UNANIMOUSLY

15.
ADJOURNMENT

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority Seeks Approval for 2026-2030 Strategic Plan
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation wants the Council to approve their plan for 2026 to 2030. This plan will guide the Housing Authority's actions and priorities for the next five years.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Construction & Development

briefs.title: UBCM 2026 Membership Dues Approved
briefs.summary: Council approved paying the 2026 membership dues for the Union of British Columbia Municipalities (UBCM). UBCM is an organization that represents local governments in BC.

briefs.title: Water Metering Technology Contract Awarded
briefs.summary: A contract for the supply and delivery of water metering technology has been awarded. This means the city has approved a specific company to provide and install new water meters.

briefs.title: Modular Housing Four-Plex Contract Approval Sought
briefs.summary: A local council is considering approving a contract for a modular four-plex housing unit. This is a request for approval, not a change that has already occurred.

briefs.title: Hired Equipment Services: Contract Awards Approved
briefs.summary: The council is set to approve 19 separate contracts for hired equipment services. This means the city will have agreements in place with various companies to rent equipment as needed.

briefs.title: Asphalt Paving Services Contract Renewal Approved
briefs.summary: A contract renewal for asphalt paving services has been approved by the council. This ensures the continuation of these services.

briefs.title: Carrigan Court Development Proposal: Council to Review Amended Plan
briefs.summary: The city council will consider working with an applicant on a revised plan for a high-density, mixed-use development at Carrigan Court. This means the project could move forward with changes after council review.
briefs.action_required: Monitor council decisions regarding the Carrigan Court development proposal.

briefs.title: Grand Promenade Mixed-Use Development: Council to Review Amended Rezoning Application
briefs.summary: The City Council will review an amended rezoning application (a request to change the permitted uses of land) for the Grand Promenade mixed-use development. If approved, the application will proceed to First and Second Reading.

briefs.title: Kingsway, Vancouver: Council to Review Proposed 12-Storey Mixed-Use Development
briefs.summary: Vancouver City Council will review a proposal for a 12-storey mixed-use building on Kingsway. The council will decide whether to move the proposal to the next stages of review.
briefs.action_required: Monitor City of Vancouver updates on the Kingsway development proposal.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Burnaby Considers Rezoning for New Self-Storage Facility on Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is considering a proposal to rezone a property on Norland Avenue. The rezoning would allow for a five-story self-storage facility to be built. Council will vote on whether to move the proposal to the next stage.
briefs.action_required: Monitor City of Burnaby updates if this project impacts your business.

briefs.title: Burnaby Council to Consider Poverty Reduction Strategy
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review and possibly approve a new strategy to reduce poverty in the city. The Burnaby Poverty Reduction Strategy aims to address the causes and effects of poverty for residents.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about the strategy's progress and potential impacts on your business.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Building Permit Activity Reports Published January and February 2026
briefs.summary: The city published reports about building permits issued in January and February 2026. These reports give the City Council information about construction happening in the city.

briefs.title: City of Burnaby: Investment Program Update and 2026 Forecast
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has released its 2025 Investment Program Year End Report and a forecast for 2026. This provides an update on the city's investments and plans for the coming year.
briefs.action_required: Review the report to understand potential impacts on your business.

briefs.title: Civic Project Status Update: Stay Informed on Local Developments
briefs.summary: The city council is providing updates on significant civic projects. This helps businesses stay informed about developments that could affect them. Knowing about these projects can help you plan for the future.
briefs.action_required: Review the civic project updates to understand potential impacts on your business.

briefs.title: Burnaby Begins Planning for 2050 Official Community Plan
briefs.summary: Burnaby is starting an "Implementation Action Plan" for its Official Community Plan (OCP). The OCP helps guide how the city will grow and change over time. The Implementation Action Plan will create specific steps to achieve the goals of the OCP.
briefs.action_required: Stay informed about opportunities to provide feedback on Burnaby's OCP.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves 20-Storey Rental Building at 5058 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby approved Bylaw #14802. It allows a 20-storey mixed-use rental building at 5058 Imperial Street. This replaces the previous plan for a six-storey building.

briefs.title: Burnaby rezoning approved for 6800 Lougheed Hwy
briefs.summary: Burnaby approved Bylaw #14803. It changes the zoning for 6800 Lougheed Hwy. This allows for a revised site plan with updated building height and size (massing).

briefs.title: Burnaby Approves Zoning Change for 49-Storey Tower on Springer Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby has approved a change to the zoning rules for 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. This change allows a developer to build a 49-storey apartment building with retail space at that location.

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends Parking Bylaw to Include EV Charging
briefs.summary: Burnaby updated its parking rules to include electric vehicle (EV) charging. The change affects the Burnaby Parking Meter and Electric Vehicle Charging Meter Bylaw 2019. This bylaw manages public EV charging stations and parking meters.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaw to understand EV charging regulations.

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends Fees for Public EV Charging Network
briefs.summary: Burnaby changed its fees for using the public electric vehicle (EV) charging network. The changes are in Bylaw #14806, which updates the Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.
briefs.action_required: Check the new fee schedule if you use Burnaby's public EV chargers.

briefs.title: Burnaby Amends Bylaw Notice Enforcement for Public EV Network
briefs.summary: Burnaby updated its bylaw enforcement rules. The change affects how the city handles violations related to its public electric vehicle (EV) charging network. This means the city can now enforce rules about using the EV charging stations.
briefs.action_required: Review Burnaby's updated bylaw enforcement rules for public EV charging.

briefs.title: Burnaby Residents Oppose Rezoning at 5058 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Residents submitted a petition opposing rezoning application REZ #25-13 for 5058 Imperial Street in Burnaby. The petition expresses concerns about the proposed changes to land use.
